sanskrit:
भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते।
रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे॥
transliteration:
bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyaṃ bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate |
rātry-āgame’vaśaḥ pārtha prabhavaty ahar-āgame ||
literal_meaning: This very multitude of beings, again and again coming-into-being, dissolves; at nightfall, O Pārtha, helplessly, and at daybreak it manifests again.
contextual_meaning: The psyche experiences itself as a repeating cycle of emergence and dissolution—thoughts, moods, identities arise with the ‘day’ and subside with the ‘night’. Fighting this rhythm creates suffering; recognizing it as impersonal cosmic law invites detachment and peace.
psychological_state: Hyper-awareness of life’s impermanence producing both existential anxiety and potential liberation from over-identification with transient roles.
trigger_scenario: Lying in bed after a breakup, watching the sunrise, realizing the same cyclical thoughts and feelings have returned despite vowing ‘never again’—the ego feels powerless against the automatic tide of mental-emotional rebirth.
anchor_text: Watch thoughts rise and fall like day and night; rest in the changeless witness.
